text: How did Vera disover she had this gift of second sight?
zh: 薇拉是如何发现自己拥有这种超感官知觉天赋的？

text: Several cases have been reported in Russia recently of people who can read and detect colours with their fingers, and even see through solid doors and walls.
zh: 俄罗斯最近报道了几起案例，讲述有人能用手指辨读和分辨颜色，甚至能透视实心的门和墙。

text: One case concerns an 11-year-old schoolgirl Vera Petrova,
zh: 其中一个案例涉及一名11岁的女学生薇拉·彼得罗娃，

text: who has normal vision but who can also perceive things with different parts of her skin, and through solid walls.
zh: 她视力正常，但还能用皮肤的不同部位感知事物，甚至能透过实心墙壁感知。

text: This ability was first noticed by her father.
zh: 这种能力最早是她父亲发现的。

text: One day she came into his office and happened to put her hands on the door of a locked safe.
zh: 有一天，她走进他的办公室，无意中把手放在一个上了锁的保险柜门上。

text: Suddenly she asked her father why he kept so many old newspapers locked away there,
zh: 她突然问父亲，为什么要把这么多旧报纸锁在里面，

text: and even described the way they were done up in bundles.
zh: 甚至还描述出报纸是如何捆扎成束的。

text: Vera's curious talent was brought to the notice of a scientific research institute in the town of Ulyanovsk, near where she lives,
zh: 薇拉这种奇特的天赋引起了她家附近乌里扬诺夫斯克市一家科研机构的注意，

text: and in April she was given a series of tests by a special commission of the Ministry of Health of the Russian Federal Republic.
zh: 四月份，俄罗斯联邦共和国卫生部一个专门委员会对她进行了一系列测试。

text: During these tests she was able to read a newspaper through an opaque screen and, stranger still,
zh: 在这些测试中，她能透过不透明的屏幕读报纸；更奇怪的是，

text: by moving her elbow over a child's game of Lotto she was able to describe the figures and colours printed on it;
zh: 她只需用肘部在儿童的乐透游戏卡上移动，就能说出上面印着的图案和颜色；

text: and, in another instance, wearing stockings and slippers,
zh: 还有一次，她穿着长袜和拖鞋，

text: to make out with her foot the outlines and colours of a picture hidden under a carpet.
zh: 仅凭脚就能辨认出藏在地毯下的一幅图画的轮廓和颜色。

text: Other experiments showed that her knees and shoulders had a similar sensitivity.
zh: 其他实验表明，她的膝盖和肩膀也具有类似的感知能力。

text: During all these tests Vera was blindfold; and indeed, except when blindfold she lacked the ability to perceive things with her skin.
zh: 在所有这些测试中，薇拉都是蒙着眼睛的；事实上，除非蒙眼，她就无法用皮肤感知事物。

text: It was also found that although she could perceive things with her fingers this ability ceased the moment her hands were wet.
zh: 研究还发现，虽然她能用手指感知物体，但只要双手一沾湿，这种能力就会立刻消失。
